task_completion.description: Open the 'Sample House' project in NCH DreamPlan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NCH DreamPlan software is open and displays a pre-built house model (the 'Sample House' or Contemporary House project) in the 3D view.

task_completion.description: Locate or import a solar panel object.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A solar panel object is selected in the object library, catalog, or is attached to the cursor ready for placement in the 3D environment.

task_completion.description: Install an array of at least four solar panels on a main roof section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view shows a minimum of 4 solar panel objects placed on top of the main roof of the house.

task_completion.description: Arrange the panels in a grid or row and ensure they follow the roof's slope.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The solar panels are neatly aligned in a row or a grid (e.g., 2x2). They are angled to match the pitch of the roof, sitting flush or parallel to the roof surface rather than floating horizontally or intersecting unnaturally.

task_completion.description: Save the project as 'solar_project.dplan' to the specified Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box showing the file name 'solar_project.dplan' and the directory 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents', or the DreamPlan application title bar displaying 'solar_project.dplan' after saving.

integrity.description: The agent used the DreamPlan graphical user interface to place the objects.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the DreamPlan 3D view, menus, or object placement tools, rather than a script or terminal window.

integrity.description: The solar panels are genuine 3D objects placed within the software's environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The panels render correctly within the 3D scene, responding to the software's lighting, shadows, and perspective, rather than being 2D image overlays pasted onto the screenshot.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's native save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The File menu or a native Windows Save dialog is visible, confirming the file was saved via the application rather than using terminal commands to rename or copy files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\solar_project.dplan. Minimum required solar panels: 4. The panels must be arranged in a grid or row and follow the roof's slope. Note: 'Solar Panel' may not be a default built-in object in DreamPlan; the agent may have imported a custom 3D model. Accept any reasonable 3D object that visually represents a solar panel.
